az-305.title: Azure AZ-305 Practice Exam
az-305.description: Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert

topics.name: Design identity, governance, and monitoring solutions
modules: Design a solution to log and monitor Azure resources

topics.name: Design data storage solutions
modules: Design a data storage solution for relational data
Design a data storage solution for semi-structured and unstructured data

topics.name: Design business continuity solutions
modules: Design a solution for backup and disaster recovery

questions.question: You are designing a multi-tier application architecture for Azure. The application requires high availability, scalability, and cost optimization. Which compute service combination would you recommend?
options: Azure Virtual Machines with Availability Sets
Azure App Service with Azure Functions for background processing
Azure Container Instances with Azure Kubernetes Service
Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets with Azure Load Balancer
questions.explanation: Azure App Service provides built-in high availability, auto-scaling, and cost optimization through consumption-based pricing for Azure Functions, making it ideal for multi-tier applications.

reasoning.correct: Azure App Service provides built-in high availability across zones, automatic scaling based on demand, and managed platform services that reduce operational overhead. Combined with Azure Functions for background processing, this offers consumption-based pricing where you only pay for actual execution time, providing excellent cost optimization. The platform handles infrastructure management, patching, and scaling automatically.

why_others_wrong.0: Azure VMs with Availability Sets provide high availability but require manual scaling configuration and constant compute costs regardless of actual usage, making them less cost-effective for variable workloads.
why_others_wrong.2: While containerized, Azure Container Instances don't provide built-in high availability and auto-scaling like App Service. AKS adds complexity and management overhead that may not be necessary for a multi-tier application.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS with Load Balancer provides good availability and scaling but still requires infrastructure management and doesn't offer the cost optimization of consumption-based pricing.

questions.question: Design a multi-tier web application with managed scaling and minimal ops. Which is best?
options: App Service for web + Functions for background jobs
AKS for web + ACI for jobs
questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

reference.title: Azure Backup and Site Recovery overview

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.
why_others_wrong.2: Single Server is regional and not designed for global multi-master.
why_others_wrong.3: Table Storage lacks global multi-master features and enterprise SLAs.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:
questions.explanation: Private Link creates private endpoints and allows disabling public network access.

reasoning.correct: Private endpoints provide private IPs and isolation for PaaS services.

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.
why_others_wrong.2: UDRs cannot make public services private.
why_others_wrong.3: App Gateway is HTTP/S proxy, not for storage endpoints.

reference.title: Azure Private Link and service endpoints

questions.question: You need an interactive dashboard combining metrics and logs with parameters and drill-down. Use:
questions.explanation: Workbooks provide parameterized, interactive visualizations across data sources.

reasoning.correct: Workbooks unify metrics/logs with templates, parameters, and drill-down.

why_others_wrong.0: Basic dashboards lack interactivity and parameters.
why_others_wrong.2: Legacy dashboards are log-only focused.
why_others_wrong.3: App Insights charts are app-centric and less flexible for multi-service views.

questions.question: A steady 5 Gbps private WAN connection to Azure with SLA is required. Which option?
questions.explanation: ExpressRoute provides private connectivity with higher throughput and SLAs.

reasoning.correct: ER uses Microsoft backbone and supports higher bandwidth options with enterprise SLAs.

why_others_wrong.0: IPsec VPN over internet cannot guarantee bandwidth or SLA.
why_others_wrong.2: P2S is for users, not site connectivity.
why_others_wrong.3: User VPN is for remote users, not WAN-scale connectivity.

questions.question: Highest durability with ability to read from secondary region during normal operations?
questions.explanation: RA-GRS provides cross-region replication with read access to secondary.

reasoning.correct: RA-GRS = GRS + read access for secondary endpoint.

why_others_wrong.0: LRS is single datacenter.
why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

questions.question: You require FIFO ordering and sessions for transactional messages. Choose:
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions and FIFO semantics for ordered message processing.

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.
why_others_wrong.2: Storage Queues lack FIFO and sessions.
why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

questions.explanation: ExpressRoute provides private connectivity with higher throughput and SLAs.

reasoning.correct: ER uses Microsoft backbone and supports higher bandwidth options with enterprise SLAs.

why_others_wrong.0: IPsec VPN over internet cannot guarantee bandwidth or SLA.
why_others_wrong.2: P2S is for users, not site connectivity.
why_others_wrong.3: User VPN is for remote users, not WAN-scale connectivity.

questions.question: Highest durability with ability to read from secondary region during normal operations?
questions.explanation: RA-GRS provides cross-region replication with read access to secondary.

reasoning.correct: RA-GRS = GRS + read access for secondary endpoint.

why_others_wrong.0: LRS is single datacenter.
why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

questions.question: You require FIFO ordering and sessions for transactional messages. Choose:
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions and FIFO semantics for ordered message processing.

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.
why_others_wrong.2: Storage Queues lack FIFO and sessions.
why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

questions.question: Design a multi-tier web application with managed scaling and minimal ops. Which is best?
options: App Service for web + Functions for background jobs
AKS for web + ACI for jobs
questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

questions.explanation: RA-GRS provides cross-region replication with read access to secondary.

reasoning.correct: RA-GRS = GRS + read access for secondary endpoint.

why_others_wrong.0: LRS is single datacenter.
why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

questions.question: You require FIFO ordering and sessions for transactional messages. Choose:
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions and FIFO semantics for ordered message processing.

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.
why_others_wrong.2: Storage Queues lack FIFO and sessions.
why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

questions.question: Design a multi-tier web application with managed scaling and minimal ops. Which is best?
options: App Service for web + Functions for background jobs
AKS for web + ACI for jobs
questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

questions.question: You require FIFO ordering and sessions for transactional messages. Choose:
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions and FIFO semantics for ordered message processing.

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.

questions.question: Design a multi-tier web application with managed scaling and minimal ops. Which is best?
options: App Service for web + Functions for background jobs
AKS for web + ACI for jobs
questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.
why_others_wrong.2: Storage Queues lack FIFO and sessions.
why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

questions.question: Design a multi-tier web application with managed scaling and minimal ops. Which is best?
options: App Service for web + Functions for background jobs
AKS for web + ACI for jobs
questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

reference.title: Azure Backup and Site Recovery overview

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.
why_others_wrong.2: Single Server is regional and not designed for global multi-master.
why_others_wrong.3: Table Storage lacks global multi-master features and enterprise SLAs.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:
questions.explanation: Private Link creates private endpoints and allows disabling public network access.

reasoning.correct: Private endpoints provide private IPs and isolation for PaaS services.

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.
why_others_wrong.2: UDRs cannot make public services private.
why_others_wrong.3: App Gateway is HTTP/S proxy, not for storage endpoints.

reference.title: Azure Private Link and service endpoints

questions.question: You need an interactive dashboard combining metrics and logs with parameters and drill-down. Use:
questions.explanation: Workbooks provide parameterized, interactive visualizations across data sources.

reasoning.correct: Workbooks unify metrics/logs with templates, parameters, and drill-down.

why_others_wrong.0: Basic dashboards lack interactivity and parameters.
why_others_wrong.2: Legacy dashboards are log-only focused.
why_others_wrong.3: App Insights charts are app-centric and less flexible for multi-service views.

questions.question: A steady 5 Gbps private WAN connection to Azure with SLA is required. Which option?
questions.explanation: ExpressRoute provides private connectivity with higher throughput and SLAs.

reasoning.correct: ER uses Microsoft backbone and supports higher bandwidth options with enterprise SLAs.

why_others_wrong.0: IPsec VPN over internet cannot guarantee bandwidth or SLA.
why_others_wrong.2: P2S is for users, not site connectivity.
why_others_wrong.3: User VPN is for remote users, not WAN-scale connectivity.

questions.question: Highest durability with ability to read from secondary region during normal operations?
questions.explanation: RA-GRS provides cross-region replication with read access to secondary.

reasoning.correct: RA-GRS = GRS + read access for secondary endpoint.

why_others_wrong.0: LRS is single datacenter.
why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

questions.question: Design a multi-tier web application with managed scaling and minimal ops. Which is best?
options: App Service for web + Functions for background jobs
AKS for web + ACI for jobs
questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

reference.title: Azure Backup and Site Recovery overview

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.
why_others_wrong.2: Single Server is regional and not designed for global multi-master.
why_others_wrong.3: Table Storage lacks global multi-master features and enterprise SLAs.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:
questions.explanation: Private Link creates private endpoints and allows disabling public network access.

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.

questions.question: A steady 5 Gbps private WAN connection to Azure with SLA is required. Which option?

questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

reference.title: Azure Backup and Site Recovery overview

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.
why_others_wrong.2: Single Server is regional and not designed for global multi-master.
why_others_wrong.3: Table Storage lacks global multi-master features and enterprise SLAs.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:
questions.explanation: Private Link creates private endpoints and allows disabling public network access.

reasoning.correct: Private endpoints provide private IPs and isolation for PaaS services.

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.
why_others_wrong.2: UDRs cannot make public services private.
why_others_wrong.3: App Gateway is HTTP/S proxy, not for storage endpoints.

reference.title: Azure Private Link and service endpoints

questions.question: You need an interactive dashboard combining metrics and logs with parameters and drill-down. Use:
questions.explanation: Workbooks provide parameterized, interactive visualizations across data sources.

reasoning.correct: Workbooks unify metrics/logs with templates, parameters, and drill-down.

why_others_wrong.0: Basic dashboards lack interactivity and parameters.
why_others_wrong.2: Legacy dashboards are log-only focused.
why_others_wrong.3: App Insights charts are app-centric and less flexible for multi-service views.

questions.question: A steady 5 Gbps private WAN connection to Azure with SLA is required. Which option?
questions.explanation: ExpressRoute provides private connectivity with higher throughput and SLAs.

reasoning.correct: ER uses Microsoft backbone and supports higher bandwidth options with enterprise SLAs.

why_others_wrong.0: IPsec VPN over internet cannot guarantee bandwidth or SLA.
why_others_wrong.2: P2S is for users, not site connectivity.
why_others_wrong.3: User VPN is for remote users, not WAN-scale connectivity.

questions.question: Highest durability with ability to read from secondary region during normal operations?
questions.explanation: RA-GRS provides cross-region replication with read access to secondary.

reasoning.correct: RA-GRS = GRS + read access for secondary endpoint.

why_others_wrong.0: LRS is single datacenter.
why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

questions.question: You require FIFO ordering and sessions for transactional messages. Choose:
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions and FIFO semantics for ordered message processing.

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.
why_others_wrong.2: Storage Queues lack FIFO and sessions.
why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.
why_others_wrong.2: Single Server is regional and not designed for global multi-master.
why_others_wrong.3: Table Storage lacks global multi-master features and enterprise SLAs.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:
questions.explanation: Private Link creates private endpoints and allows disabling public network access.

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.

why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

reference.title: Azure Backup and Site Recovery overview

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.
why_others_wrong.2: Single Server is regional and not designed for global multi-master.
why_others_wrong.3: Table Storage lacks global multi-master features and enterprise SLAs.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:
questions.explanation: Private Link creates private endpoints and allows disabling public network access.

reasoning.correct: Private endpoints provide private IPs and isolation for PaaS services.

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.
why_others_wrong.2: UDRs cannot make public services private.
why_others_wrong.3: App Gateway is HTTP/S proxy, not for storage endpoints.

reference.title: Azure Private Link and service endpoints

questions.question: You need an interactive dashboard combining metrics and logs with parameters and drill-down. Use:
questions.explanation: Workbooks provide parameterized, interactive visualizations across data sources.

reasoning.correct: Workbooks unify metrics/logs with templates, parameters, and drill-down.

why_others_wrong.0: Basic dashboards lack interactivity and parameters.
why_others_wrong.2: Legacy dashboards are log-only focused.
why_others_wrong.3: App Insights charts are app-centric and less flexible for multi-service views.

questions.question: A steady 5 Gbps private WAN connection to Azure with SLA is required. Which option?
questions.explanation: ExpressRoute provides private connectivity with higher throughput and SLAs.

reasoning.correct: ER uses Microsoft backbone and supports higher bandwidth options with enterprise SLAs.

why_others_wrong.0: IPsec VPN over internet cannot guarantee bandwidth or SLA.
why_others_wrong.2: P2S is for users, not site connectivity.
why_others_wrong.3: User VPN is for remote users, not WAN-scale connectivity.

questions.question: Highest durability with ability to read from secondary region during normal operations?
questions.explanation: RA-GRS provides cross-region replication with read access to secondary.

reasoning.correct: RA-GRS = GRS + read access for secondary endpoint.

why_others_wrong.0: LRS is single datacenter.
why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

questions.question: You require FIFO ordering and sessions for transactional messages. Choose:
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions and FIFO semantics for ordered message processing.

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.
why_others_wrong.2: Storage Queues lack FIFO and sessions.
why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

questions.question: Design a multi-tier web application with managed scaling and minimal ops. Which is best?
options: App Service for web + Functions for background jobs
AKS for web + ACI for jobs
questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

reference.title: Azure Backup and Site Recovery overview

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.
why_others_wrong.2: Single Server is regional and not designed for global multi-master.
why_others_wrong.3: Table Storage lacks global multi-master features and enterprise SLAs.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:
questions.explanation: Private Link creates private endpoints and allows disabling public network access.

reasoning.correct: Private endpoints provide private IPs and isolation for PaaS services.

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.
why_others_wrong.2: UDRs cannot make public services private.
why_others_wrong.3: App Gateway is HTTP/S proxy, not for storage endpoints.

reference.title: Azure Private Link and service endpoints

questions.question: You need an interactive dashboard combining metrics and logs with parameters and drill-down. Use:
questions.explanation: Workbooks provide parameterized, interactive visualizations across data sources.

reasoning.correct: Workbooks unify metrics/logs with templates, parameters, and drill-down.

why_others_wrong.0: Basic dashboards lack interactivity and parameters.
why_others_wrong.2: Legacy dashboards are log-only focused.
why_others_wrong.3: App Insights charts are app-centric and less flexible for multi-service views.

questions.question: A steady 5 Gbps private WAN connection to Azure with SLA is required. Which option?
questions.explanation: ExpressRoute provides private connectivity with higher throughput and SLAs.

reasoning.correct: ER uses Microsoft backbone and supports higher bandwidth options with enterprise SLAs.

why_others_wrong.0: IPsec VPN over internet cannot guarantee bandwidth or SLA.
why_others_wrong.2: P2S is for users, not site connectivity.
why_others_wrong.3: User VPN is for remote users, not WAN-scale connectivity.

questions.question: Highest durability with ability to read from secondary region during normal operations?
questions.explanation: RA-GRS provides cross-region replication with read access to secondary.

reasoning.correct: RA-GRS = GRS + read access for secondary endpoint.

why_others_wrong.0: LRS is single datacenter.
why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

questions.question: You require FIFO ordering and sessions for transactional messages. Choose:
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions and FIFO semantics for ordered message processing.

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.
why_others_wrong.2: Storage Queues lack FIFO and sessions.
why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

questions.question: Design a multi-tier web application with managed scaling and minimal ops. Which is best?
options: App Service for web + Functions for background jobs
AKS for web + ACI for jobs
questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.
why_others_wrong.2: Single Server is regional and not designed for global multi-master.
why_others_wrong.3: Table Storage lacks global multi-master features and enterprise SLAs.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:
questions.explanation: Private Link creates private endpoints and allows disabling public network access.

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.
why_others_wrong.2: UDRs cannot make public services private.

questions.question: You need an interactive dashboard combining metrics and logs with parameters and drill-down. Use:
questions.explanation: Workbooks provide parameterized, interactive visualizations across data sources.

reasoning.correct: Workbooks unify metrics/logs with templates, parameters, and drill-down.

why_others_wrong.0: Basic dashboards lack interactivity and parameters.

questions.question: A steady 5 Gbps private WAN connection to Azure with SLA is required. Which option?
questions.explanation: ExpressRoute provides private connectivity with higher throughput and SLAs.

reasoning.correct: ER uses Microsoft backbone and supports higher bandwidth options with enterprise SLAs.

why_others_wrong.0: IPsec VPN over internet cannot guarantee bandwidth or SLA.
why_others_wrong.2: P2S is for users, not site connectivity.
why_others_wrong.3: User VPN is for remote users, not WAN-scale connectivity.

questions.question: Highest durability with ability to read from secondary region during normal operations?
questions.explanation: RA-GRS provides cross-region replication with read access to secondary.

why_others_wrong.0: LRS is single datacenter.
why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

questions.question: You require FIFO ordering and sessions for transactional messages. Choose:
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions and FIFO semantics for ordered message processing.

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.
why_others_wrong.2: Storage Queues lack FIFO and sessions.
why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.
why_others_wrong.3: Backups alone do not meet low RPO/RTO or orchestration requirements.

reference.title: Azure Backup and Site Recovery overview

questions.question: You need globally distributed, multi-region write database with millisecond latency SLA. Which service?
options: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server
questions.explanation: Cosmos DB offers turnkey multi-master, multi-region writes with SLAs on latency/availability.

reasoning.correct: Cosmos DB provides tunable consistency and multi-region, multi-master replication.

why_others_wrong.0: SQL DB supports geo-replication but not multi-master writes.
why_others_wrong.2: Single Server is regional and not designed for global multi-master.
why_others_wrong.3: Table Storage lacks global multi-master features and enterprise SLAs.

questions.question: You must block public access to Storage while enabling private access from VNets. Choose:
questions.explanation: Private Link creates private endpoints and allows disabling public network access.

reasoning.correct: Private endpoints provide private IPs and isolation for PaaS services.

why_others_wrong.0: Service endpoints still expose a public endpoint.
why_others_wrong.2: UDRs cannot make public services private.
why_others_wrong.3: App Gateway is HTTP/S proxy, not for storage endpoints.

reference.title: Azure Private Link and service endpoints

questions.question: You need an interactive dashboard combining metrics and logs with parameters and drill-down. Use:
questions.explanation: Workbooks provide parameterized, interactive visualizations across data sources.

reasoning.correct: Workbooks unify metrics/logs with templates, parameters, and drill-down.

why_others_wrong.0: Basic dashboards lack interactivity and parameters.
why_others_wrong.2: Legacy dashboards are log-only focused.
why_others_wrong.3: App Insights charts are app-centric and less flexible for multi-service views.

questions.question: A steady 5 Gbps private WAN connection to Azure with SLA is required. Which option?
questions.explanation: ExpressRoute provides private connectivity with higher throughput and SLAs.

reasoning.correct: ER uses Microsoft backbone and supports higher bandwidth options with enterprise SLAs.

why_others_wrong.0: IPsec VPN over internet cannot guarantee bandwidth or SLA.
why_others_wrong.2: P2S is for users, not site connectivity.
why_others_wrong.3: User VPN is for remote users, not WAN-scale connectivity.

questions.question: Highest durability with ability to read from secondary region during normal operations?
questions.explanation: RA-GRS provides cross-region replication with read access to secondary.

reasoning.correct: RA-GRS = GRS + read access for secondary endpoint.

why_others_wrong.0: LRS is single datacenter.
why_others_wrong.1: ZRS is zonal within a region.
why_others_wrong.2: GRS lacks read access to secondary in normal operation.

questions.question: You require FIFO ordering and sessions for transactional messages. Choose:
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions and FIFO semantics for ordered message processing.

reasoning.correct: Service Bus queues/topics offer sessions, transactions, and dead-lettering with rich semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Event Hubs is a big data streaming ingestion service.
why_others_wrong.2: Storage Queues lack FIFO and sessions.
why_others_wrong.3: Event Grid is for push-based event routing, not queueing.

questions.question: Design a multi-tier web application with managed scaling and minimal ops. Which is best?
options: App Service for web + Functions for background jobs
AKS for web + ACI for jobs
questions.explanation: App Service provides PaaS features; Functions provide serverless background processing with consumption pricing.

reasoning.correct: Managed platform, auto-scale, and lower operational burden suit solution architecture goals.

why_others_wrong.0: VMs require higher ops overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: AKS adds complexity not required for this scenario.
why_others_wrong.3: VMSS still requires VM management and lacks PaaS features.

questions.question: For a global app requiring fast failover and WAF, which fronting service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Front Door offers global anycast, fast failover, and integrated WAF.

reasoning.correct: Edge presence and health probes reduce failover times and improve global performance.

why_others_wrong.0: DNS-based and slower failover.
why_others_wrong.2: Regional-only and not global POP-based.
why_others_wrong.3: Basic LB lacks global routing and WAF features.

questions.question: Which Azure Policy effects are useful to enforce storage security baselines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All listed effects can participate in governance for storage baseline compliance.

reasoning.correct: Audit monitors, Deny blocks, DeployIfNotExists configures, Modify remediates, Append adds required settings/tags.

questions.question: For VM workload requiring orchestrated region failover with minimal RPO/RTO, what should you choose?
questions.explanation: ASR provides replication, runbooks, and orchestrated failover with low RPO/RTO.

reasoning.correct: ASR replicates VMs and supports planned/unplanned failover and test failover.

why_others_wrong.0: Backup provides recovery, not continuous replication.
why_others_wrong.2: ZRS is storage redundancy, not a DR orchestration solution.

questions.question: You need to grant time-bound privileged access to Azure subscriptions with approval workflow and audit trail. What should you implement?
options: Azure AD Privileged Identity Management with eligible role assignments
Azure RBAC permanent assignments with custom roles
Azure Policy to deny privileged operations
questions.explanation: PIM provides just-in-time elevation, approvals, MFA, and auditing for privileged roles.

reasoning.correct: PIM supports eligible roles, activation with approval, MFA, and access reviews for least privilege.

why_others_wrong.1: Permanent assignments do not provide JIT, approval, or time-bound access.
why_others_wrong.2: Azure Policy cannot provide on-demand elevation for human identities.
why_others_wrong.3: Deny assignments at management groups do not solve JIT elevation needs.

reference.title: What is Azure AD Privileged Identity Management?

questions.question: Which risk signals can be used in Conditional Access to require MFA? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Risk-based policies use User risk and Sign-in risk signals from Azure AD Identity Protection.

reasoning.correct: Risk signals (user and sign-in risk) are part of Identity Protection and can trigger MFA or block.

why_others_wrong.2: Device compliance is an access condition, not a risk signal.
why_others_wrong.3: Named locations are not risk signals; they are location conditions.

questions.question: You must allow contractors from an external Azure AD tenant to access only one internal application using their identities. What is the best approach?
options: Azure AD B2B collaboration with guest users and app assignment
Create new cloud-only user accounts for each contractor
Establish AD DS forest trust between tenants
questions.explanation: B2B collaboration brings external users as guests to your tenant and limits access via app assignment.

reasoning.correct: Guest users authenticate with their home tenant and receive least-privilege access to specific apps.

why_others_wrong.1: Cloud-only accounts duplicate identities and increase overhead.
why_others_wrong.2: Forest trusts do not apply to Azure AD tenants.
why_others_wrong.3: Lighthouse is for cross-tenant management, not end-user app access.

reference.title: What is Azure AD B2B collaboration?

questions.question: You need app-consistent backups for Azure VMs with daily retention for 35 days. What should you configure?
options: Recovery Services vault with a backup policy using VSS
questions.explanation: Azure Backup in a Recovery Services vault supports app-consistent backups using VSS and retention policies.

reasoning.correct: Backup policy in Recovery Services vault enables scheduling, app-consistency, and retention.

why_others_wrong.1: Automation snapshots lack retention governance and app-consistency.
why_others_wrong.2: ASR is for replication/DR, not scheduled backups.
why_others_wrong.3: Blob snapshots are per-disk and not integrated with VM backup policies.

questions.question: How can you protect backups from accidental or malicious deletion in Azure Backup?
options: Enable soft delete and multi-user authorization (MUA) for critical operations
Enable NSG rules on the subnet
Use RBAC Reader role for backup admins
questions.explanation: Soft delete retains deleted backup data for a retention period. MUA requires secondary approval for high-risk operations.

reasoning.correct: Soft delete and MUA increase protection against ransomware and malicious deletions.

why_others_wrong.1: NSGs do not protect vault operations.
why_others_wrong.2: Reader role does not manage backup or protect deletion.
why_others_wrong.3: Audit-only policies do not enforce protective controls.

questions.question: You must back up SQL Server running on Azure VMs with item-level restore and long-term retention. What should you use?
options: Azure Backup for SQL Server in Azure VMs
File-level backup using Azure Files Share snapshots
questions.explanation: Azure Backup provides application-aware backups for SQL on VMs with PITR and granular restore.

reasoning.correct: The SQL workload backup supports full/diff/log backups and granular restore.

why_others_wrong.1: File share snapshots are not database-aware.
why_others_wrong.2: BACPAC is for schema/data export, not backup/restore strategy.
why_others_wrong.3: ASR is for replication, not backups.

reference.title: Back up SQL Server on Azure VMs

questions.question: Which option provides protection against a full datacenter failure within a region for VMs?
questions.explanation: Availability Zones distribute resources across physically separate datacenters within a region.

reasoning.correct: Zones provide datacenter-level fault isolation.

why_others_wrong.0: Sets protect only against rack-level failures.
why_others_wrong.2: PPGs reduce latency; they do not provide HA.
why_others_wrong.3: Accelerated Networking improves throughput/latency.

questions.question: You need zone-resilient SQL with 99.99% SLA. What should you deploy?
options: Azure SQL Database Business Critical with zone redundancy
SQL on VMs with Availability Set
questions.explanation: Business Critical tier supports zone redundancy for high availability.

reasoning.correct: BC tier provides multiple replicas and zone redundancy.

why_others_wrong.0: General Purpose uses remote storage and may not be zone redundant.
why_others_wrong.2: VM-based solution requires complex setup and lacks PaaS SLA features.
why_others_wrong.3: Single-instance MI is not zone redundant by default.

reference.title: High availability for Azure SQL Database

questions.question: You require L7 load balancing with zone redundancy and WAF for regional traffic. Which service should you choose?
options: Application Gateway v2 (WAF) with zone redundancy
questions.explanation: App Gateway v2 supports zone redundancy and WAF for regional L7 load balancing.

reasoning.correct: It provides HTTPS termination, path-based routing, autoscaling, and WAF with zone redundancy.

why_others_wrong.0: Basic LB is L4 and not zone redundant.
why_others_wrong.1: Traffic Manager is DNS-based, not L7 LB.
why_others_wrong.3: Front Door is global; the requirement is regional.

questions.question: What is the typical sequence for migrating VMware VMs with Azure Migrate Server Migration?
options: Discover → Assess → Replicate → Test migrate → Migrate
questions.explanation: Discovery, assessment, replication, test migration, then production migration is the recommended sequence.

reasoning.correct: Assessment informs sizing and readiness before replication and migration.

why_others_wrong.0: Replication should not precede discovery and assessment.
why_others_wrong.2: Assessment requires discovery first.
why_others_wrong.3: Assessment should precede replication.

questions.question: Which component must you deploy in your environment to collect discovery data for Azure Migrate?
options: Azure Site Recovery Mobility service only
questions.explanation: The Azure Migrate appliance collects metadata for discovery and assessment.

reasoning.correct: Appliance performs discovery of servers and dependencies.

why_others_wrong.1: ExpressRoute is optional connectivity, not required.
why_others_wrong.2: Agent alone is insufficient for discovery scope.
why_others_wrong.3: Mobility service is used for replication, not discovery.

reference.title: Discover servers with Azure Migrate appliance

questions.question: You must migrate a mission-critical SQL Server database to Azure SQL Managed Instance with minimal downtime. What should you use?
options: Azure Database Migration Service online migration
Backup and restore to Azure Blob Storage
Transactional replication to Azure SQL Database
questions.explanation: DMS online migration supports continuous data sync and cutover with minimal downtime.

reasoning.correct: Online migration reduces downtime by syncing changes until cutover.

why_others_wrong.1: BACPAC incurs long downtime and is for small/medium databases.
why_others_wrong.2: Backup/restore is not supported for MI without managed backup integration and still incurs downtime.
why_others_wrong.3: Transactional replication targets Azure SQL Database, not MI for all scenarios.

certificationInfo.title: Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert
certificationInfo.description: This certification validates your expertise in designing cloud and hybrid solutions that run on Microsoft Azure, including compute, network, storage, monitoring, and security.
prerequisites: Azure Administrator Associate (AZ-104) or Azure Developer Associate (AZ-204)
Advanced experience and knowledge of IT operations
Experience in virtualization, networking, identity, and security

skillsMeasured.category: Design identity, governance, and monitoring solutions (25-30%)
skills: Design identity and access management solutions
Design data protection and disaster recovery solutions
Design data archiving and retention solutions
Design backup and disaster recovery solutions
